scope: A group second-round results, forecast audit, table impact, and model adjustment handoff.

sources.name: ESPN Mexico vs South Korea match centre
AP Mexico vs South Korea report
The Guardian Czechia vs South Africa live
Al Jazeera Czechia vs South Africa live

standings_after_round.status_note: must improve game management before final match

matches.postmortem_file: 比赛/已完成比赛/小组赛/A组/2026-06-18_墨西哥_1-0_韩国_复盘.md

forecast_audit.wdl: missed draw lean; Mexico win probability was too low
forecast_audit.scoreline: near miss; one-goal low-event script correct, Korea goal overestimated
forecast_audit.script: risk-management script hit, but Mexico's 1-0 conversion was underweighted

model_adjustments: apply possession_to_xg discount for Korea against physical low-block/pressing teams
reduce draw_acceptance weight when host can qualify with a narrow win

matches.postmortem_file: 比赛/已完成比赛/小组赛/A组/2026-06-18_捷克_1-1_南非_复盘.md

forecast_audit.wdl: missed Czechia win lean; draw warning was valid
forecast_audit.scoreline: Czechia one-goal output hit; South Africa late scoring path underweighted
forecast_audit.script: Czechia game-management risk correctly identified but not penalized enough

main_lessons: Low-event matches can still favor the host/favorite if first-goal conversion and defensive management are strong.
Czechia's aerial and set-piece edge should be separated from its ability to close matches.
South Korea possession volume must be discounted when shot quality is low.
South Africa should retain a late-draw path in survival games even when attacking baseline is modest.
handoff_to_round3: Mexico final-round motivation and rotation must be modeled separately after qualification.
South Korea remain favored against South Africa but need finishing-quality checks.
Czechia vs Mexico must combine Czechia urgency with Mexico rotation and home-field control.
